mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series is in its top quartile, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 200 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate consumption and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real gross domestic product growth confirms macroeconomic expansion, directly increasing corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during recovery phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when the economy is in recovery, or the signal reverses direction during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in out-of-sample validation.
